tsTroubleshooting.domains: (topic tsTroubleshooting) (topic hi-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic ut-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic oce-d) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c tsTroubleshooting oceTroubleshooting-c)
tsTroubleshooting.title: 25704 Media thickness is out of range

abstract.p: Media thickness is out of range.
The media thickness is out of range. Printing with the selected media profile is blocked to prevent undesired print quality issues.

If this happens when loading a media with a media profile defined in a RIP, check the media thickness setting in the RIP. This error will be generated when this media thickness value is left at it's default value of 800 µm. The media thickness has to be set to the actual thickness of the media and has to be lower than 800 µm.

If it happens using a Calibration media, go to SDS 25-1-004 (Vinyl), 25-1-005 (Film) or 25-1-006 (Paper) respectively and set it to the correct media thickness of the loaded media. Enter the media width by taping below the media type on touch user interface.
See 12 Use of the Service media profiles
